howItWorks.intro: This fall home maintenance cost calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: HVAC cost based on service type (tune-up ~$150, repair ~$550)
Gutter cost scales with home size: $225 base x (sqft / 2000)
Sealing cost = (Base $40 + $2/year of home age) x DIY multiplier
Cost of Skipping estimate = Total Maintenance x 3.5
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates. Consult a professional for critical decisions.

faq.question: What fall home maintenance should I prioritize?
faq.answer: The highest-priority tasks are: furnace inspection, gutter cleaning, weatherstripping doors and windows, and disconnecting outdoor hoses. Skipping furnace maintenance risks carbon monoxide issues and costly mid-winter breakdowns.

faq.question: How much does fall home maintenance cost on average?
faq.answer: Most homeowners spend $300-800 on fall maintenance. Costs increase with home size, age, and if you hire professionals. The investment prevents much larger winter repair bills.

faq.question: When should I start fall home maintenance?
faq.answer: Start in September before the rush. HVAC companies get booked up in October, and you want weatherization done before the first freeze. Schedule furnace service earliest since technicians fill up fast.

faq.question: Is fall maintenance worth the cost?
faq.answer: Absolutely. For every $1 spent on preventive maintenance, you typically avoid $3-5 in emergency repairs. A $150 furnace tune-up can prevent a $3,000+ mid-winter furnace replacement.
